## [00:00 - 01:19]

Do you have any tips on how we can communicate value to people that seem above us or older without being an arrogant or a pushover? First of all, you're a great communicator. In the way you just asked me that question, you have great communication skills. It's a couple of things. And I think the first thing is be prepared in those communications with those who are older and have higher status than you. I think if you want them to respect you and see you as being confident, always be prepared in those moments where you're communicating with them. And then when it comes to communication, specific techniques, one thing us Australians have to look out for is never ending your sentences on a high pitch. Hey, great to see you. Thank you for spending time with me. Be really careful. Hey, thanks. Thanks for spending time with me. So that radiates confidence, pausing, which you did fantastic. More than this, remove all non words and filler words. Arms and arms, rob you of credibility like nothing else. And I feel one more thing that really makes people who are in higher positions really respect you is when you are a really good listener. When you are a really good listener and you truly listen to what they're saying, and then you take what they're saying and you immediately apply it. There's nothing that makes someone want to spend more time with you, especially mentor figures than that. So if you're prepared and your delivery is very calm and well-mannered, that's going to allow you to come across with a lot of confidence.
